data.title: UK Judge Dismisses $770M Bitcoin Landfill Hard Drive Case: A Lesson for Crypto Investors
data.description:
A UK judge has dismissed the long-standing legal case of James Howells, who sought access to a landfill containing a hard drive with Bitcoin worth more than $770 million. This ends the 12-year saga for the IT engineer from Newport, Wales that captivated the cryptocurrency community all these years.

Back in 2009, Howells mined 8,000 Bitcoin, which was valued very low back then but is now trading around $94,600, with the stage set for a major BTC comeback after recent price drops.

However, Howells’ former partner mistakenly disposed of the hard drive that contained his private keys in 2013, which rendered the funds inaccessible. Since then, he has sought legal action to obtain permission to excavate the Newport landfill. He even offered a share of the recovered Bitcoin to the local council.

James Howells Loses 12-Year Legal Battle

Although Howells made repeated requests and put forward his proposal for excavation, the Newport City Council declined his appeals citing logistical and environmental concerns. The final blow came when Circuit Commercial Judge Keyser ruled out the case on the grounds of “no realistic prospect” to succeed. He remarked the search as looking for a needle in a haystack and was simply not viable.

“Anything that goes into the landfill goes into the council’s ownership,” said the judge.

Howells expressed his frustration after losing the 12-year long legal battle, claiming that the authorities had not considered his case properly. However, he acknowledged that the court confirmed his ownership of the Bitcoin. Now, he is left to explore alternative options like tokenization to monetize his digital assets.

The Howells case serves as a precautionary tale for global crypto investors. It demonstrates the importance of securely storing digital assets. Cryptocurrencies are different from traditional financial systems and need private keys for access. Losing these keys may result in the permanent loss of the assets.

Keep Your Crypto Safe with Best Wallet

This case is a wake-up call for those investing in cryptocurrency, whether that’s the likes of Bitcoin and Ethereum or one of the best meme coins. Digital solutions like Best Wallet are, therefore, a must-have for every investor. It offers a secure cloud-based platform, eliminating the risks associated with physical storage devices like hard drives or USBs.

Crypto wallets come with features like automatic backups, multi-device synchronization, and robust security protocols. Best Wallet, in this context, ensures that investors never face catastrophic losses like Howells. Investors prioritizing safety and convenience must adopt a modern wallet as a smart step to protect their crypto assets in the digital economy.

With Best Wallet, you can buy, store, sell, deposit, stake, and manage your crypto holdings in a single place. Plus, you can track your portfolio in real-time with quick overviews of all your transactions. The platform goes a step ahead and also offers market insights and key crypto market updates so that you can stay updated at all times.

It’s worth noting that there’s also $BEST, which is Best Wallet’s proprietary token. The token is currently in presale. Holding $BEST gives you early access to crypto presales, reduced transaction fees, and $BEST airdrops. The wallet itself is easy to use and non-custodial, plus you do not need any KYC to trade in cryptocurrency.

data.title: Albania to offer 122 mln euro of 1-yr T-bills on Jan 14
data.description:
TIRANA (Albania), January 10 (SeeNews) - Albania’s finance ministry will offer 12 billion leks ($125.9 million/122.3 million euro) of one-year Treasury bills on January 14, central bank data showed.

Some 3.48 billion leks of the offering is reserved for the central bank, according to a notice published by the central bank.

The T-bills will mature on January 15, 2026.

At the last auction of one-year T-bills held on December 30, the finance ministry sold 8.66 billion leks worth of debt paper, below its 10.5-billion-leks target. Average weighted yield came in at 2.76%, slightly higher than the yield of 2.73% achieved at the previous auction of one-year Treasury bills held on December 24.

(1 euro = 98.14 leks)

data.title: Ambu tops STOXX on outlook hike, strong Q1 prelims
data.description:
** Shares in Danish medical devices maker Ambu jump 13% to the top of the pan-European STOXX 600 after it posted preliminary Q1 results and hiked it full-year outlook

** It now targets 2024/25 organic revenue growth of 11-14% vs previous outlook of 10-13%

** It sees 2024/25 EBIT margin before special items at 13-15% vs 12-14% previously

** It reports preliminary Q1 revenue of DKK 1.51 bln ($208.4 mln) and EBIT before special items of DKK 243 mln

** "The high-end of Ambu's new guidance implies consensus estimates going up ~10%, below today's share price reaction and also comes on top of positive momentum in the stock in recent days," ABG Sundal Collier analyst Morten Larsen says

** "But strong Q1 alleviates a lot of fears e.g. making the new guidance range looking cautious, and alleviate fears over recent weakening growth in the Pulmonology endoscope business and how much Ambu may need to boost OPEX spending," he adds

** If the gains hold, the shares are on track for their best day since 2021

($1 = 7.2468 Danish crowns)

data.title: Medigen Vaccine Biologics Grants Vaccine Distribution Rights to French Firm
data.description:
Medigen Vaccine Biologics (TPE:6547) signed a distribution deal with France's Substipharm Biologics for its ENVACGENR Enterovirus A71 (EV-A71) vaccine.

EV-A71 is a severe enterovirus affecting children and causes hand, foot, and mouth disease and herpangina, according to a Thursday Taiwan Exchange filing.

Under the deal, Substipharm Biologics was granted exclusive rights for the Vietnamese market and first refusal rights for Thailand, the Philippines, Indonesia, Malaysia, and Singapore.

The collaboration will boost the vaccine's introduction into Asia-Pacific and ASEAN markets with high demand, it noted.

data.title: Update: Market Chatter: Alphabet's Google Facing Privacy Class Action Trial After Judge Rejects Dismissal
data.description:
(Updates with Google's comment in the fourth paragraph.)

Alphabet's Google must face a privacy class action lawsuit after a federal judge declined to dismiss the action, paving the way for a potential jury trial in August, Reuters reported late Wednesday.

The lawsuit alleges that Google collected personal data from users' cell phones after they switched off a button to stop the tracking, violating California privacy laws, according to Reuters.

US District Judge Richard Seeborg rejected the argument that the company adequately disclosed how its Web & App Activity settings worked and that users consented to the tracking, according to the report.

Google told MT Newswires that "privacy controls have long been built into our service and the allegations here are a deliberate attempt to mischaracterize the way our products work."

The trial is set for Aug. 18, the report added.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Mercedes-Benz 2024 car sales fall in tough year for automakers
data.description:
Mercedes-Benz's core car sales fell in 2024, a tough year for the auto industry marked by waning demand in a weak economy, especially in key market China, the German luxury carmaker said on Friday.

It sold 1,983,400 cars during the year, down 3% versus 2023, weighed down by a 7% drop in China and a 3% decline in Europe.

Annual sales of battery-electric cars (BEV) dropped by 23% to 185,100 vehicles, adding pressure on the carmaker as new, harsher EU CO2 emission reduction targets take effect this year, which could potentially mean costly pooling deals or hefty fines for Mercedes if the BEV sales don't pick up.

The automaker cut its full-year profit margin target twice in 2024 and said it will step up cost cuts, joining a growing number of European rivals blaming a weakening Chinese car market for falling profits and margins.

Mercedes also plans to lower its mid-term profitability targets as market conditions are unlikely to improve in the near future, a source familiar with the matter told Reuters on Tuesday.

The carmaker is due to report full-year 2024 financial results on Feb. 20.

data.title: 5 Themes to Watch as the Stock Market Kicks Off 2025 — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Connor Smith

After posting annual gains north of 20% for the second year in a row, the stock market is off to a decent start in 2025, and strategists at Barclays offered five key themes to watch.

The S&P 500 is up 0.6% to start the year, while the Nasdaq Composite is up 0.9%. The Dow Jones Industrial Average is up 0.2%.

Barclays strategist Venu Krishna and his colleagues point out the S&P 500's two-year run ending this past year was one of its best ever, gaining 23.3% in 2024 after rising 24.2% in 2023.

The two-year compound annual growth rate of just under 24% is good for its fifth-best in the last 50 years, Krishna writes.

"While we see no significant long-term relationship between 2Y price return CAGR and SPX returns over the subsequent year, there were only 3 years at the end of which the 2Y price return CAGR exceeded 20% and the rally did not decelerate the following year: 1996, 1997 and 2020," Krishna writes.

Krishna is also paying close attention to last year's unusual seasonality. The S&P 500 posted strong gains in the first three quarters of 2024, but the fourth quarter was its weakest. Both a steady third quarter performance and a weak fourth quarter defy historical trends, Krishna writes. Historically, there has been a positive correlation between fourth-quarter returns and returns in the subsequent first quarter.

"When 4Q performance is worse than median (as it was most recently), the subsequent 1Q median return is flat to slightly negative," Krishna writes. "If history is any guide, SPX performance could be muted in 1Q25, a sharp contrast to 2024 when equities entered the year with significant momentum."

The third major theme was continued dominance among the tech sector, while discretionary and financials led the rest of the pack.

Returns in those sectors, Krishna writes, "were less dependent on multiple expansion than gains elsewhere in the index thanks to earnings upside, but Financials and Tech are nonetheless trading nearly as expensive as they've ever been over the past decade."

Krishna and his team are also worried about market breadth, calling concentration a key risk in 2025.

"The continued dominance of mega-cap Tech means that a sustainable broadening of US equity upside remains elusive," Krishna writes. "The percentage of SPX constituents beating the index rose to the highest level in over a year by October, but fell sharply over the final 2 months of 2024 as Materials and Healthcare performance cratered."

The last big theme for Krishna is the fact that U.S. stocks look expensive relative to Europe. The MSCI Europe is trading at 13.7 next-12-months estimated earnings per share, compared with 22.6 times for the S&P 500.

"While this could make European equities look attractive on a relative basis, we believe there are fundamental reasons underpinning this valuation gap," Krishna writes.

He points out U.S. GDP growth has strongly outpaced Europe in the past two years. Barclays strategists expect U.S. GDP growth to slow down to 2.1% in 2025, compared with 0.7% for Europe.

"This should underpin US EPS growth that continues to exceed that of the EU," Krishna writes.

Write to Connor Smith at connor.smith@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Heimstaden AB (publ) Contemplates Issuance of New Senior Unsecured Notes and Announces Tender Offer for Certain SEK and EUR Senior Unsecured Notes
data.description:
Heimstaden

Mandate for Fixed Income Investor Meetings

Heimstaden AB (publ) (“Heimstaden”) has mandated J.P. Morgan S.E., Nordea Bank Abp, Pareto Securities AS, and Swedbank AB (publ) as joint arrangers and bookrunners to arrange a series of fixed income investor meetings commencing on 13 January 2025, to explore the possibility to issue new SEK and/or EUR denominated senior unsecured notes with an expected tenor of around 3–5 years (the “New Notes”). Subject to, inter alia, market conditions, a capital markets transaction may follow.

Tender Offer

In conjunction with the contemplated issue of the New Notes, Heimstaden is today launching an offer to the holders of its SEK 500 million floating rate notes due April 2025 with ISIN XS2469914308 (of which SEK 158 million is currently held by Heimstaden) (the “SEK Apr-25 Notes”), SEK 1,200 million floating rate notes due October 2025 with ISIN SE0014991352 (of which SEK 290 million is currently held by Heimstaden) (the “SEK Oct-25 Notes”) and EUR 350 million fixed rate notes due March 2026 with ISIN SE0015657903 (the “EUR Notes”) (together referred to as the “Notes”), to tender their Notes for purchase by Heimstaden at a fixed purchase price of 100.00% (the “Purchase Price”) of the nominal amount of the respective Notes, up to a capped amount equal to the total nominal amount of new SEK and EUR senior unsecured notes, the New Notes, following the tender offer (the “Transaction Cap”) as further described in the Tender Information Document as defined below. In addition to the Purchase Price, Heimstaden will pay accrued but unpaid interest up to (but excluding) the settlement date for the tender offer (referred to as the “Tender Offer”). 

The Tender Offer will be conditional upon the successful issue of the New Notes and subject to the terms and conditions described in the tender information document dated 10 January 2025 attached to this press release (referred to as the “Tender Information Document”).

The terms and conditions of each of the Notes contain clean-up calls that will be available for exercise by Heimstaden subject to, among other things, reaching the required threshold in respect of the Notes. The required threshold in respect of (i) the SEK Apr-25 Notes is 75% of the aggregate principal amount of the SEK Apr-25 Notes outstanding; (ii) the SEK Oct-25 Notes is 80% of the total nominal amount of the SEK Oct-25 Notes; and (iii) the EUR Notes is 80% of the total nominal amount of the EUR Notes. In addition, the terms and conditions of the SEK Apr-25 Notes contain a par call option that is available for exercise by Heimstaden at any time from (and including) 14 October 2024 to (but excluding) their maturity date.

Subject to the New Financing Condition (as defined in the Tender Information Document) being met, and if the required thresholds and other conditions in accordance with the terms and conditions of the respective Notes are met, the Issuer may exercise the call options described above in respect of any Notes not accepted for purchase pursuant to the Tender Offers.

The purpose of the Tender Offer is, among other things, to proactively improve Heimstaden’s liability composition and debt maturity profile.

The Tender Offer expires at 15:00 CET on 16 January 2025, unless extended, re-opened, withdrawn, or terminated at the sole discretion of Heimstaden. Settlement of the Tender Offer is expected to occur on or around 31 January 2025.

The terms and conditions of the Notes will continue to be in effect for noteholders who do not participate in the Tender Offer and/or whose Notes are not accepted for repurchase by Heimstaden.

The full terms and conditions of the Tender Offer and participation instructions can be found in the Tender Information Document attached to this press release. Noteholders are advised to read carefully the Tender Information Document in full.

Dealer Managers on the Tender Offer:

J.P. Morgan SE, liability_management_EMEA@jpmorgan.com, +44 20 7134 2468

Nordea Bank Abp, nordealiabilitymanagement@nordea.com, +45 6161 2996

Swedbank AB (publ), liabilitymanagement@swedbank.se

Contacts:

Frederik Berling, Media

+45 2130 9489, media@heimstaden.com

Cody Nelson, Investor Relations

+47 948 94 196, ir@heimstaden.com

About Heimstaden:

Heimstaden is a leading European residential real estate manager and investor with around 162,000 homes across nine countries with a property value of SEK 332 billion. We acquire, develop, and manage properties with an evergreen perspective. Guided by our Scandinavian heritage and values Dare, Care, and Share – we fulfil our mission to enrich and simplify our customers’ lives through Friendly Homes. Heimstaden is listed on Nasdaq First North Growth Market. Read more at www.heimstaden.com. Certified Adviser is Carnegie Investment Bank AB (publ).

This information is such information that Heimstaden AB (publ) is obliged to publish in accordance with the EU Market Abuse Regulation. The information was submitted, through the agency of the above contact persons, for publication on 10 January at 09:30 CET

https://news.cision.com/heimstaden/r/heimstaden-ab--publ--contemplates-issuance-of-new-senior-unsecured-notes-and-announces-tender-offer-%2Cc4089889

data.title: Thor Explorations Says Drilling Outlines High-grade Gold Potential at Nigerian Mine
data.description:
Thor Explorations said Friday it intersected high-grade gold mineralization in a recent diamond drilling program at the Segilola mine in Nigeria.

The program is targeting a potential down-plunge of the Segilola ore body beyond the existing open pit design, the London-listed miner said, adding that the results suggest the potential for the extension of Segilola resource both along strike to the south and at depth.

Thor Explorations plans to continue drilling to improve its understanding of the Segilola mine's mineralization.

data.title: Kepler upgrades Legrand to 'buy', sees return to growth in 2025
data.description:
** Kepler Cheuvreux raises Legrand to "buy" from "hold", expecting the French electrical and digital building infrastructure company to return to growth in 2025

** The analysts say Legrand should benefit from structural demand for data centre white space product, growing needs in segments linked to the energy transition, and a cyclical recovery in residential and non-residential construction markets

** They note the shares have underperformed the main indices and have de-rated vs closer peers

** Kepler adds the company's business model remains as strong as ever, with market-leading positions in attractive niche segments set to continue underpinning its best-in-class profitability and cash conversion

** Out of 21 analysts that cover Legrand, 12 rate the stock "strong buy" or "buy", ​six "hold" and three​ "strong sell" or "sell"

data.title: Sterling Trims Losses After U.K. Treasury Reiterates Fiscal Pledges — Market Talk
data.description: Sterling trims losses sustained Thursday following a sharp rise in U.K. government bond yields as a U.K. Treasury spokesperson said meeting the fiscal rules is non-negotiable. That means Treasury chief Rachel Reeves could deliver some fiscal consolidation if the updated Office for Budget Responsibility forecasts in March show the government isn't on track to meet fiscal rules, ING's Francesco Pesole says in a note. "The market seems to be acknowledging the Treasury's reiterated fiscal pledges and this has prevented the gilt and pound selloffs from becoming disorderly." GBP/USD falls 0.2% to 1.2288 after hitting a 14-month low of 1.2239 Thursday, according to FactSet. EUR/GBP rises 0.1% to 0.8280 after reaching a two-month high of 0.8407 Thursday.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

data.title: China, HK stocks end the week lower as investors await fresh stimulus
data.description:
China and Hong Kong stocks ended the week lower as traders held off on increasing their market investments and awaited fresh stimulus measures from Beijing.

** China's blue-chip CSI300 Index and the Shanghai Composite Index both closed down around 1.3% on Friday. Hong Kong benchmark Hang Seng was down 0.9%.

** This week, the CSI300 Index has fallen by 1.1%, while the Hang Seng Index declined 3.5%.

** "The key question in 2025 is how much stimulus policymakers will provide. It will largely depend on the impact from tariffs, as policymakers will do just enough to achieve the GDP growth target," analysts, led by Larry Hu at Macquarie, said in a note.

** "Few investors feel it's a bull market, as corporate earnings remain soft amid weak domestic demand. The prevailing view is that liquidity will get looser in 2025 but nominal growth will stay sluggish."

** Real estate and AI-related (.CSI930713) shares led the decline in the onshore market, down 2.1% and 2.6%, respectively.

** Country Garden has proposed a deal to its offshore creditors that will cut its debt by $11.6 billion, paving the way for the property developer to seek more time from the high court in Hong Kong to implement a restructuring plan.

** Vanke's onshore shares fell 3.7% on Friday, and its bond prices have been dropping since December last year. The company said on Thursday that it is fully committed to addressing its public debt maturing this year.

** China's central bank said on Friday it has suspended treasury bond purchases, triggering a jump in yields and spurring speculation that the move was aimed at defending a falling currency.

data.title: ASE Technology Reports 6% Growth in December Revenue
data.description:
ASE Technology Holding reported Friday net revenue of 52.91 billion New Taiwan dollars ($1.63 billion) in December, up 6% from a year earlier.

Revenue increased by 1% year over year to NT$162.26 billion in Q4 and by 2.3% to NT$595.41 billion for full-year 2024.

data.title: Suzlon Energy Wins GST Penalty Appeal
data.description:
Suzlon Energy said the Appeal Commissionerate, Ahmedabad, India has ruled in its favor, overturning a penalty of 841,120 Indian rupees imposed by the Joint Commissioner, Gujarat Central GST Department, according to a Thursday filing with the Indian bourses.

The pre-deposited amount will be re-credited to the company, Suzlon Energy said in the filing.

data.title: COMMENT-GBP sees BoE caught between a rock and a hard place
data.description:
The Bank of England may be damned if it cuts interest rates next month, and damned if it doesn't, with little upside for sterling.

A BoE rate reduction in February could prompt further selling of the pound - which might help fan the UK inflation flames.

However, a BoE rate hold on Feb. 6 may keep the UK economy flatlining, and make the country's fiscal outlook ever more perilous, also to the detriment of sterling.

BoE Deputy Governor Sarah Breeden said on Thursday that recent evidence supported the case to cut rates gradually but that it was tricky to gauge the right speed of easing.

Breeden also said this week's rise in gilt yields, which depressed GBP/USD to a 14-month low of 1.2239 on Thursday, was linked to global factors.

British households' expectations for inflation rose in December, according to a Citi/YouGov survey published on Thursday.

Related comments:

For more click on

data.title: Moldova Inflation Rate at Over 1-Year High
data.description:
The annual inflation rate in Moldova accelerated to 7% in December 2024, from 5.4% in the prior month.

This marked the highest reading since September 2023, as prices increased sharply for services (10.5% vs 3.3% in November).

Meanwhile, inflation slowed for both non-food (4.6% vs 4.8%) and food products (7.2% vs 7.5%), particularly for vegetables (-2.8%) and fresh fruits (-3%).

On a monthly basis, consumer prices rose by 1%, the highest since April, following a 0.3% gain in the preceding period.

data.title: Rheinmetall Buys Majority Share in Software Developer Blackned
data.description:
(08:06 GMT) *Rheinmetall Increases Share Ownership in Blackned to 51% From 40%
(08:06 GMT) *Rheinmetall: Transaction Subject to Approval
(08:07 GMT) *Rheinmetall Electronics Acquired the Additional Shares
(08:07 GMT) *Rheinmetall: Blackned Specialises in Software for the Digitalisation of Armed Forces
(08:08 GMT) *Rheinmetall Did Not Provide Financial Details of the Share Acquisition
(08:25 GMT) Rheinmetall Buys Majority Share in Software Developer Blackned

By Pierre Bertrand

Rheinmetall's electronics subsidiary increased its stake in Blackned, giving it a 51% controlling interest in the Bavarian software developer.

Bremen-based Rheinmetall Electronics previously held a 40% stake, the German arms maker said.

The acquisition is subject to approval by the German Federal Cartel Office, the company said.

Blackned specializes in software for the digitalization of armed forces, Rheinmetall said. It didn't disclose financial details of the acquisition.

Write to Pierre Bertrand at pierre.bertrand@wsj.com

data.title: From Wildfires to Firewalls: BlackRock et al Leave Climate Coalitions
data.description:
As wildfires blaze in California and a fierce storm brews in North Carolina, Wall Street’s climate pledges go up in smoke as BlackRock and others back out of climate conventions before Trump’s inauguration.

California Wildfires Highlight Increasing Climate Disasters

Los Angeles is in flames—again. Wildfires have claimed at least ten lives, scorched thousands of homes and businesses, and wreaked havoc in California neighborhoods like upscale Pacific Palisades.

Reuters
@Reuters

The most destructive wildfires ever for Los Angeles may cause billions of dollars in insured losses, ratings agencies said, though many homes are likely uninsured https://t.co/DjxTgwGtYO pic.twitter.com/lSFIddauTN

Jan 10, 2025

Famous names like Jamie Lee Curtis and Billy Crystal are among the victims who lost their homes in the inferno. Officials traced the fire’s origins to a wooded arroyo behind a house on Piedra Morada Drive, but lightning—often the usual suspect—was ruled out early. The fires, driven by relentless winds, underscore California’s ongoing battle with climate-fueled catastrophes.

North Carolina: Storm Clouds and Stark Realities

While California fights fires, North Carolina braces for a storm the likes of which of the state hasn’t seen in decades. North Carolina is preparing for a massive storm predicted to bring serious amounts of snow and ice, enough to cut power in the middle of the winter. As the storm looms, residents are being urged to prepare to as Duke Energy, the local power provider, warns that half an inch of ice could well damage power lines.

Climate extremes are becoming the norm, and the stark contrast between the fire-scorched West and the storm-battered East couldn’t be clearer. But as nature rages on both coasts, Wall Street has quietly retreated from its climate commitments.

Wall Street’s Climate Backpedal: Banks Bail on Net-Zero

In a move as subtle as a fire alarm in a windstorm, major U.S. institutions—including BlackRock—have withdrawn from net-zero alliances just days before Trump’s inauguration. The Net Zero Banking Alliance, a UN-backed coalition that commits financial institutions to align lending and investment portfolios with net-zero emissions by 2050, has lost some of its biggest players. JP Morgan has become the latest major bank to exit the UN-backed Alliance, joining Citigroup, Bank of America, Morgan Stanley, Wells Fargo, and Goldman Sachs. All six institutions have withdrawn since early December.

Prof Michael E. Mann
@MichaelEMann

I discussed the role that human-caused climate change is playing in the devastating Southern California wildfires with @BriKeilarcnn on @CNN earlier today: pic.twitter.com/aTRGsZdDMD

Jan 08, 2025

BlackRock, meanwhile, have announced that they intend to leave the Net Zero Asset Managers Initiative. The Initiative is a global coalition of asset managers dedicated to achieving net-zero greenhouse gas emissions by 2050 or earlier. This commitment aligns with their fiduciary responsibilities to clients and beneficiaries and supports global efforts to limit temperature increases to 1.5 degrees Celsius. The initiative also promotes investment strategies consistent with the goal of reaching net-zero emissions within the same timeframe.

BlackRock, the world’s largest asset manager, cited mounting political and legal pressures as reasons for its exit. ESG (Environmental, Social, and Governance) investing, once a golden ticket for Wall Street, has become a lightning rod for criticism from conservative lawmakers and regulators.

The timing? Impeccable. The decision? Controversial. The message? Climate commitments are optional when profits are on the line.

California, Climate, and Cash: A Toxic Triangle

The irony is rich—almost as rich as the banks themselves. While California burns, North Carolina braces for impact, Wall Street’s retreat from climate pledges sends a clear signal: climate change is bad for business.

Reuters
@Reuters

Damage and economic loss from the California wildfires, one of the worst in history, could exceed $50 billion, AccuWeather estimated https://t.co/6BMs4CiH0H pic.twitter.com/yeIjUHWLgr

Jan 09, 2025

California’s wildfires are already wreaking havoc on insurers, utility companies, and real estate markets. Homeowners in fire-prone areas are seeing skyrocketing insurance premiums or losing coverage altogether. The fires are not just environmental disasters; they’re economic ones, too.

BlackRock’s decision to bail on net-zero comes as it faces shareholder scrutiny and political pushback over its ESG practices. For all the talk of green energy and climate responsibility, the financial sector’s actions speak louder than its marketing slogans.

Storms Ahead: What’s Next for Climate Finance?

With Wall Street retreating and natural disasters escalating, the future of climate finance is murky at best. BlackRock and other banks argue that they’re still committed to sustainability, just not under the constraints of alliances like Net Zero. Critics, however, see this as a clear abdication of responsibility.

Flames, Floods, and Financial Follies

California’s wildfires and North Carolina’s storms are not just weather events—they’re wake-up calls. Yet Wall Street seems to have hit the snooze button. As BlackRock and its peers step back from climate commitments, the disconnect between environmental urgency and financial priorities has never been starker.

For now, the fires rage, the storm looms, and Wall Street watches from the sidelines, calculating the cost of doing nothing.

For more stories around the edge of finance, follow our Trending section.

data.title: Hong Kong Shares Fall 0.9%; Eyes on U.S. Data — Market Talk
data.description: Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index closed 0.9% lower at 19064.29, erasing earlier gains. Investors are waiting for U.S. nonfarm payrolls data due later, which could influence the Federal Reserve's rate cut trajectory. Expectations around the Fed's rate path affect appetite for riskier assets like stocks. Chinese property developer Sunac China slumped 26% after the company received a winding up petition. Other decliners include JD Health International, which dropped 5.1%, and Lenovo Group, which lost 4.9%. Meanwhile, Country Garden Services rose 5.2% and Zijin Mining Group added 3.1%. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Hang Seng Sinks 3.5% This Week
data.description:
The Hang Seng fell 177 points or 0.9% to close at 19,064 on Friday, declining for the fifth session and reaching its lowest in over six weeks due to broad-based sector losses.

Nervous traders reacted to the PBoC’s decision to pause treasury bond purchases which triggered a rise in yields.

Investors cautiously awaited China’s December trade data, due over the weekend.

In November, exports grew less than expected, while imports unexpectedly shrank.

Meanwhile, US futures pointed to a weaker open on Wall Street, with traders bracing for NFP data later today.

For the week, the index slumped 3.4%, marking its second straight weekly drop amid mounting concerns that Chinese stocks were nearing bear-market territory.

Additionally, the latest FOMC meeting minutes suggested persistent inflation risks, potentially capping the scope for rate cuts this year.

Among single stocks, steep losses included JD Health Intl. (-4.8%), Meituan (-3.3%), Kunlun Energy Co. (-3.1%), and China Tower Corp. (-2.7%).

data.title: Airbus rises after stable yearly deliveries data
data.description:
** Shares in Airbus rise 1.4% after the world's largest plane maker on Thursday reported 2024 deliveries roughly in line with market expectations and looked certain to maintain leadership of the jetmaking industry

** "A potential FY25 delivery guidance in line with our current assumption of 819 deliveries looks achievable," Deutsche Bank says

** Airbus' Q1 deliveries are likely to be weak due to few aircraft in inventory, but they will pick up in Q2 as the new LEAP HPT blades are built into new engines, alleviating engine shortages, Citi says

** Citi lifts its 2024 core earnings forecast for Airbus by 4% and raises its PT by 14% to 209 euros

** By 0811 GMT, the stock is up at 158.7 euros and among top performers on France's CAC 40 index, which is flat

($1 = 0.9713 euros)

data.title: SRF, Navin Fluorine slip upto 4% as investors book profits after recent surge
data.description:
Shares of specialty chemical players--SRF and Navin Fluorine slipped as much as 4 percent on January 10 as investors rushed to cash in profits off the counters after the recent surge in the stocks. While SRF zoomed nearly 14 percent, Navin Fluorine soared around 10 percent in the previous session, giving investors plenty headroom to book profits.

At 1.41 pm, shares of Navin Fluorine were trading with cuts of nearly 4 percent at Rs 3,689.80 on the NSE, while those of SRF were down around 3 percent at Rs 2,601.95. With today's fall, both the stocks also snapped their three-day winning streak.

The surge in the two stocks in the previous session were fueled by reports of an increase in prices of refrigerant gases by US gas distributors. IGas USA stated that the supply of R32 and R125 was been affected which resulted in an uptick in prices by up to 200 percent.

Follow our market blog to catch all the live action

The price hike for refrigerant gases bodes extremely well for the margins of specialty chemical makers like SRF and Navin Fluorine. Brokerage firm Equirus noted that a $1 per kilogram increase in realisations of the R32 refrigerant could boost SRF's EBITDA by Rs 260 crore. Similarly, Navin Fluorine's EBITDA could improve by Rs 77 crore for every $1 per kilogram rise in R32 gas realisations.

SRF has a production capacity of around 29,000 to 30,000 tonnes for R32, while Navin Fluorine's capacity stands at around 4,500 tonnes, with plans to double it by adding an additional 4,500 tonnes next month. Furthermore, SRF also has a capacity of around 7,000 tonnes for the R125 refrigerant.

The development also came as an unexpected boost for the two companies, especially since the chemical sector has faced earnings pressure over the past two years due to subdued global demand and an influx of Chinese inventory. As a result, the positive news was well-received by investors.Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Market Chatter: Samsung's Harman International Unveils Advanced Automotive Intelligence at CES 2025
data.description:
Samsung Electronics' automotive and audio subsidiary Harman International unveiled its new "automotive intelligence" solutions at the CES 2025 on Wednesday, featuring personalized in-cabin experiences through AI and contextual awareness.

Key highlights comprise the "Luna" avatar, an AI-powered assistant integrated with Harman's Ready Vision QVUE AR head-up display, and "Ready Aware," which uses digital twins and machine learning to enhance situational awareness, The Korea Herald reported Thursday.

Shares of Samsung Electronics fell more than 1% at market close on Friday.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Shenwu Energy Saving's Subsidiary Signs 1 Billion Yuan Battery Plant Contract
data.description:
Shenwu Energy Saving's subsidiary, Jiangsu Metallurgical Design Institute, signed a 1 billion yuan contract with power transmission maintenance company Zhongqing Advanced Battery Manufacturing (Shihezi), according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange disclosure on Thursday.

Jiangsu Metallurgical will construct a 6-gigawatt high-efficiency battery plant for Zhongqing Advanced Battery Manufacturing.

The project will have a land area of 94666.08 square meters and its construction is expected to start on Jan. 18 and end on March 31, 2026, the Chinese machine-made paper and adhesive manufacturer said.

The company's shares closed 9% lower.

data.title: Sterling slides for a fourth day amid bond market turmoil
data.description:
Sterling continued its slide on Friday, its fourth day of falls, as elevated global bond yields kept the currency under pressure.

The pound was down 0.17% against the dollar at $1.2286 , hovering close to Thursday's 14-month low of $1.2239.

Global borrowing costs have soared amid concerns about rising inflation, reduced chances of a drop in interest rates, and uncertainty over how U.S. President-elect Donald Trump will conduct foreign or economic policy.

This has propped up the dollar while sending ripples through other currencies and stocks.

Among the worst hit has been the UK market, with sterling having lost 1.9% since Tuesday.

The yield on British government bonds, or gilts, soared this week, driving the government's borrowing costs to their highest in over 16 years.

This is putting pressure on finance minister Rachel Reeves, potentially forcing her to cut future spending.

Benchmark 10-year gilt yields (GB10YT=RR) edged up in early trading on Friday to around 4.84%, but remained below Thursday's high of 4.925%, their highest since 2008.

But sterling continued to slide, as investor concern mounted about the country's finances.

"There remains clear concern over the likelihood that all of the Chancellor's fiscal headroom has now been eaten up by the sell-off in gilts, and the anaemic nature of UK economic growth," said Michael Brown, strategist at Pepperstone.

Traders are paying more to hedge against big swings in the pound than at any time since the March 2023 banking crisis.

One-month options volatility, a measure of demand for protection, hit a high of 10.9% on Thursday (GBP1MO=).

By Friday morning, this had retreated marginally to 10.08%.

Investors are now looking ahead to key U.S. jobs data published later in the session, to confirm their view that U.S. rates could stay higher for longer, which could cement the dollar's rally further.

data.title: France Household Spending Recovers in November
data.description:
Household consumption in France increased by 0.3% month-on-month in November 2024, rebounding from a downwardly revised 0.3% decline in October.

The recovery was primarily driven by higher consumption of engineered goods (+0.9% vs. -1.3% in October), durable goods (+1.9% vs. -0.4%), and other engineered goods (+0.2% vs. -0.1%).

In contrast, food consumption grew at a slower pace (0.3% vs. 1.3% in October), while energy consumption continued to decline (-0.8% vs. -1.2%), largely due to a further drop in electricity usage.

data.title: Spain Industrial Output Shrinks in November
data.description:
Industrial production in Spain fell by 0.4% year-on-year in November 2024, following a downwardly revised 1.5% increase in the prior month.

This marked the first decline in industrial activity after three consecutive months of growth, on lower production of capital goods (-2.8%) and energy (-1.9%).

On a seasonally adjusted monthly basis, industrial output shrank by 0.8% in November.

data.title: Alibaba May Report Accelerated 3Q Sales Growth — Market Talk
data.description: Alibaba could post accelerated growth in gross merchandise volume for fiscal 3Q compared with fiscal 2Q as its market share has continued to stabilize, HSBC analysts say in a note. Both China's trade-in program and the November shopping festival supported the company's accelerated GMV growth, the analysts say. Competition from ByteDance's Douyin and Tencent's WeChat should remain manageable, the analysts say, as they think Alibaba will remain committed to investing in user experience to defend market share. "Continual monetization improvement in China e-commerce coupled with recovery in earnings growth thereafter is crucial for a share price re-rating," the analysts say. HSBC projects the e-commerce giant's fiscal 3Q revenue to grow 8% on year. HSBC maintains a buy call on Allibaba with its ADR target price unchanged at $128.00. The ADRs last closed at $83.69. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)
